name: GMAT (General Mission Analysis Tool)
description: NASA's free, open-source mission analysis tool for trajectory design, orbit determination, and mission planning. Used on real NASA missions including LCROSS, LADEE, and OSIRIS-REx.
whyUseIt: GMAT is the only free tool with the fidelity and validation pedigree of commercial mission planning software. It handles interplanetary trajectories, low-thrust propulsion, and complex multi-body gravity models. Being open-source, you can inspect and understand every algorithm.

vocabulary.definition: Algorithm that numerically integrates equations of motion to predict future positions
GMAT's optimisation tool that adjusts parameters to achieve desired orbital conditions

beginnerMistakes: Starting with complex missions before mastering simple two-body problems
Not understanding the coordinate frames GMAT uses
Ignoring convergence tolerances in the targeter
